metadata.description: Sulis AI plugin marketplace — methodology studios, requirements analysis, facilitation, context cartography, right-sized architecture, disciplined change primitives, deterministic code intelligence, founder coordination, and autonomous Work Package execution end-to-end. v1.89.0 lands Phase 5 #5 of the change-as-primitive build — the terminal-launcher port (sulis v0.45.0 → v0.46.0). Ports ae_task_executor/terminal_launcher.py (504 LOC) into plugins/sulis/scripts/_terminal_launcher.py (~280 LOC) per ADR-001's strip+adapt+drop matrix: input validators + _build_launch_script (MUC-2 env-scrub preamble, SULIS_CHANGE_ID export, shlex-quoted extra_env, MUC-1 injection guard) + platform dispatchers (_launch_macos via osascript, _launch_linux via gnome-terminal→konsole→xterm shutil.which order with NFR-4 honest failure, _launch_headless) + launch_change_terminal entry-point (launch.sh 0o755 + session.json persistence under ~/.sulis/changes/{id}/) + pre_prompt delivery via quoted HERE-DOC (ADR-003 — single-quoted SULIS_PROMPT_EOF tag disables bash expansion; _validate_pre_prompt rejects tag-collision + >50KB). New _change_context.py (write_change_context) writes the pre-spawn recon CONTEXT.md (change identity + git state + per-primitive next-step hint covering all 22 primitives + 3 CC fallbacks; pure-read, never mutates the repo). sulis-change start gains --spawn + --intent: composes recon (unconditional) + pre-prompt build + launch_change_terminal; spawn failure is non-fatal (branch/worktree/metadata/recon survive; spawn_result.status surfaces the failure). write_change_metadata whitelist extended to persist change_id/handle/intent (fixes the latent change_id drop resolve_current_change depended on). Sulis agent body gains a 'Change context (when SULIS_CHANGE_ID is set)' section (verify-via-resolve_current_change / read CONTEXT.md / greet in change-context mode; stale-env three-option honesty; unset no-op). Stdlib only (NFR-5). 68 new unit tests (50 launcher + 10 recon + 8 spawn-composition; subprocess+platform mocked) + 5 manual smoke docs under tests/manual/. v1.88.0 implements RC v0.3.0 — the repo-profiles model (sulis v0.44.0 → v0.45.0). The Repository Contract Standard is rewritten (734 → 1405 lines) to add three profiles on one MECE discriminator (deployable-web-app — today's strict contract, byte-for-byte unchanged; published-artifact — libraries/CLIs/marketplaces that validate-not-deploy; internal-tool — the repo is the deliverable) PLUS first-class multi-artifact composition (a repo declares an artifacts: list; rules compose by UNION so a deployable artifact keeps its full strict set and a co-located library can never subtract from it) PLUS an orthogonal contribution_model axis (team → merge queue MUST; solo → MUST-NOT, direct merge per GIT-05). The load-bearing non-weakening guarantee: the deployable-web-app column is identical to v0.2.0 in every row — profiles relax a MUST only where it is physically meaningless for that profile, never for deployable. Ships the RC-02 deadlock fix in the standard text + tooling: classic required checks on dev = branch-ci ONLY; merge-queue-ci is the queue's internal merge_group gate (team-only), never a classic required check (it deadlocked queue entry — found live during the inaugural pipeline dogfood). wpx-arrival-check rewritten to replace the deploy_target:none hack with the profile-applicability matrix (13 mock_gh-driven unit tests; live ok:true against this repo). This repo migrated to profile: published-artifact + contribution_model: solo (the deviations block retired — the three relaxations are now defined profile behaviour, not exceptions); the dev-merge-queue ruleset deleted; the vestigial merge-queue-ci.yml removed. Fixed-versioning only (one tag, many outputs); independent per-artifact versioning deferred (would break the one-dev/one-main/one-promotion invariant). Backward-compat: a repo with no profile + no contribution_model defaults to deployable-web-app + team = the unchanged strict v0.2.0 contract. Design records (DESIGN.md + ADR-001..004) live under .architecture/repo-profiles/ (gitignored design scratch); the standard text is the committed authoritative artifact. v1.87.0 bootstraps the repository contract for this marketplace repo (sulis v0.43.1 → v0.44.0) so the executor's Step 0 arrival check can run. Authors the six RC-13 workflow files (branch-ci / merge-queue-ci / deploy-staging / health-and-smoke / promote-dev-to-main / release-prod) adapted to the plugin-marketplace profile — CI runs the real pytest suite + manifest validation; the deploy/health slots run manifest-version-drift + plugin-primitive-load checks instead of a URL deploy (a marketplace has no deploy target — the dev branch IS the installable surface, a SemVer tag on main IS the release). Adds .github/CODEOWNERS (RC-10), .sulis/repo-contract.yml (project-specific command capture + documented deviations: RC-05/06/08 deploy-secret + tag-signing MUSTs downgrade to WARN under deploy_target: none — the gap the deferred RC v0.3.0 'repo profiles' work will formalise), and .sulis/bootstrap-repo-contract.sh (the admin gh-script the OWNER runs to apply branches + protections + merge queue + environments + tag protection — RC-09 forbids the executor token from holding admin, so it's surfaced not fired). Authors plugins/sulis/scripts/wpx-arrival-check — the RC-11 Step 0 verifier specified in the standard but never built; verifies RC-01..RC-10 via gh api + filesystem, emits the RC-11 JSON contract (ok/errors/warnings), exit 0/2/1, downgrades deploy-coupled MUSTs to WARN under the marketplace profile. 5 new mock_gh-driven unit tests green; live invocation produces the honest pre-bootstrap delta. Triggered by the founder choosing 'make repo conformant first' when /sulis:run-all couldn't run on the marketplace repo. One pre-existing unrelated failure (wpx-train cmd_run LOC debt, HD-001) noted not fixed. v1.86.0 patches add-agent to v0.2.0 (sulis v0.42.0 → v0.43.0) — five gate updates landed against the meta-skill that authors agents: Gate 1 adds the Specialist Boundary Analysis sub-step for coordinator/router agents (produces a binding table mapping artifact classes → owning specialists + dispatch mechanism); Gate 2 adds three declarative frontmatter blocks lifted from studios/explorer.yaml — `context_sources:` (files loaded at startup with required + purpose), `delegation:` (artifact_owners map + dispatch_via map + authorisation policy, binding on the agent's behaviour), `routes_to:` (specialist routing targets with founder-intent triggers); Gate 3 adds tier-aware body-size budget (LIGHT 150 / STANDARD 300 / HEAVY 500 lines target; 1.5× hard ceiling with mandatory `## Why this is big` rationale paragraph) + per-section `> Standards:` citation header rule (cite, don't restate); Gate 4 adds two new perspectives — Delegation Discipline (4-check scoring whether the agent CAN delegate correctly) + Body Density Conformance (4-check scoring whether Gate 3 body-size + citation rules are honoured); Gate 5 adds MUC-A5 Specialist-Bypass misuse case (coordinator authors specialist-owned artifact directly instead of dispatching — the failure mode that drove v0.2.0, observed mid-session when Sulis authored WP-005..WP-007 directly instead of dispatching back to SEA's plan-work). The patch is foundation for re-running add-agent against plugins/sulis/agents/sulis.md in deepening mode to close the 10 issues surfaced in the self-audit (delegation policy hidden in prose; 1827-line body restating 5 standards; 3 competing self-checks; stale specialist references post-consolidation; identity buried at line 323; no Required reading section; verification spiral with only 2 custom dimensions missing the disciplines the body declares as MUST). v1.85.0 lands Phase 5 partial of the change-as-primitive build — the executor-side infrastructure that makes Phase 4's standards amendments operational code. Sulis v0.41.0 → v0.42.0. Four atoms shipped: (1) ULID + 6-char handle generator (Crockford-base32; inline, zero-deps); sulis-change start now populates change_id + handle in .changes/{primitive}-{slug}.yaml. (2) SULIS_CHANGE_ID env-var binding + resolve_current_change() + back_integrate_change_branch() helpers in _wpxlib.py — the merge-not-rebase mechanism per CW-04 with 5 structured statuses (already_current / merged_ok / merge_conflict / fetch_failed / internal_error). (3) wpx-pipeline Step 0 + Step 12.5 wire-up via --change-worktree-path flag (covers the --force-single hotfix path). (4) wpx-train Step 0 + Step 12.5 wire-up (covers the default per-batch path). The full per-WP shipping flow now invokes auto back-integration at the lifecycle boundaries Phase 4 codified. 21 new unit tests (13 ULID + 8 helpers; mocked subprocess); 109/109 in the relevant test subsets. SQLite schema (#4) + terminal launcher (#5) deferred — they're operator-experience layers; they don't gate executor correctness. v1.84.0 landed Phase 4 of the change-as-primitive build — 4 standards amendments codify what Phase 5's infrastructure will populate: WORK_PACKAGE_STANDARD v1.1.0 adds `change_id:` field to WP-01 Identity; change-work-standard v0.2.0 adds CW-04 Auto back-integration subsection (merge-not-rebase with two trigger points + structured conflict handling); repository-contract-standard v0.2.0 clarifies RC-04 merge-queue source = `change/*` branches (not `feat/*` directly); lifecycle.md gains Step 0 (arrival check) + Step 12.5 (post-WP back-integration). All amendments backwards-compatible. The four amendments compose: Step 0 + Step 12.5 are the executor's per-step touchpoints that operationalise CW-04's auto back-integration; the merge-queue routing in RC-04 ensures the change branch (not individual WP branches) reaches the queue; the `change_id:` field links every WP to its parent change. Phase 5 (the change-as-primitive infrastructure) implements what these amendments codify. v1.83.0 landed Phase 3 fourth and final consolidation — sulis-security (v0.6.0 → v0.7.0 [DEPRECATED]) consolidated into sulis (v0.39.0 → v0.40.0). Trivial scope: 1 skill (codebase-assess, already-DEPRECATED, kept name), 1 agent (security-reviewer), 1 reference (viability-framework). bulk_rewrite.py applied 52 substitutions across 32 files. First run benefiting from the v0.1.2 move-then-sweep ordering discipline — single combined commit (no fix-forwards needed; the sea-run bugs are now prevented by the encoded ordering). Phase 3 of the change-as-primitive build is now COMPLETE: all four specialist plugins (sulis-context, srd, sea, sulis-security) have been folded into the canonical sulis plugin. Marketplace surface reduced to one front-door plugin per the change-as-primitive design. Phases 4-7 of the design remain (standards amendments, change-as-primitive infrastructure, founder-facing skills, end-to-end test). v1.82.0 patched consolidate-into-sulis to v0.1.2 (sulis v0.39.0) — two highest-leverage signals from sea consolidation shipped as fixes: (1) bulk_rewrite.py packaged as first-class helper (after 3 ad-hoc usages in srd_sweep.py + sea_sweep.py + early sulis-context manual sweep — promotes ad-hoc python scripts to a permanent helper with JSON-driven replacement table + dry-run mode + smoke-tested); (2) move-then-sweep ordering encoded as non-negotiable in SKILL.md Commit 4 + Gotchas (move ALL source content BEFORE running bulk_rewrite.py, otherwise self-references inside the about-to-move content survive into the new location — bug bit twice during sea, requiring 2 fix-forward commits). 4 lower-leverage signals deferred: tier-gating asymmetry in compare_baseline.py, scanner-reported path stability across moves, atomic-per-step combined-commit guidance, tin-test heuristic refinements. v1.81.0 landed Phase 3 third consolidation — sea (v0.20.0 → v0.21.0 [DEPRECATED]) consolidated into sulis (v0.37.0 → v0.38.0). 8 skills moved with 5 tin-test renames (blueprint/decompose/harden/probe/verify → draft-architecture/plan-work/harden-codebase/analyse-codebase/verify-architecture); 1 agent moved (engineering-architect); 11 references moved (boring-code, change-primitives, code-review-standard, mece-3-architecture, red-green-blue, etc.). External ref sweep via ad-hoc python script: 327 substitutions across 70 files. v1.80.0 landed Phase 3 second consolidation of the change-as-primitive build — srd (v1.22.0 → v1.23.0 [DEPRECATED]) consolidated into sulis (v0.36.0 → v0.37.0). 5-commit chain (6ed9e9b / f99482b / cd7e2e9 / wrap-up) — 6 skills moved with 3 tin-test renames (spec-index/srd-templates/tree-synthesis → index-specifications/requirements-templates/map-architecture); 1 agent moved (requirements-analyst); 13 references moved (the marketplace-wide cross-cutting standards: AAF / FE / change-work / convention-preference / engineering-principles / executor-loop / founder-english / git-workflow / pr-hygiene / repository-contract / security / cognitive-load / content-quality / coaching-without-conflict); 25 example specifications moved to plugins/sulis/docs/srd-specifications/. External ref sweep across 60 files with 149 substitutions including 8 in CLAUDE.md, 16 in plugins/sulis/agents/sulis.md, 6 in 6 cross-plugin specialist agents. consolidate-into-sulis v0.1.1 helper scripts caught all 333 refs (151 original external + ~180 internal cross-refs in moved files) without manual git grep — the v0.1.1 slash-command pattern fix prevented the ~75% miss rate observed in the sulis-context run. v1.79.0 patched consolidate-into-sulis to v0.1.1 (sulis v0.36.0) — six recipe-improvement signals captured from the sulis-context run land as concrete fixes: find_external_refs.py adds /{source}: slash-command pattern (caught 75% of refs missed in v0.1.0); compare_baseline.py signature function now prefers `identifier` + `extras.rule` to catch code-health rule IDs (fixes the PH-103 false attribution); SKILL.md adds a gotcha about staging Edits before git commit; sub-step 0d uses 2>/dev/null to separate stderr from stdout; external-ref-sweep.md adds .architecture/**/*.md as category 13; SKILL.md documents Commit 1 no-op handling. v0.1.1 ships before the next Phase 3 consolidations (srd → sea per founder direction; ordering deviates from design doc to do largest second after sulis-context calibration run). v1.78.0 landed Phase 3 first consolidation of the change-as-primitive build — sulis-context (v0.3.1 → v0.4.0 [DEPRECATED]) consolidated into sulis (v0.34.0 → v0.35.0). 5-commit chain (0e5c9ea / 584d438 / 2348bc5 / c4f6358 / wrap-up). 3 skills tin-test-renamed (discover/refresh/show → discover-context/refresh-context/show-context); 1 agent moved (context-cartographer); 3 references moved (classification-taxonomy, context-index-template, discovery-protocol); external ref sweep across 9 marketplace files for both path patterns (caught by find_external_refs.py) and slash-command patterns (caught manually — script gap surfaced for v0.1.1 patch). Gate 0 code-health baseline captured (70 pre-existing findings, tier-2 hard-stop on critical security finding). VERIFICATION_REPORT.md for the run lives at plugins/sulis/skills/consolidate-into-sulis/runs/sulis-context-2026-05-25/. First real exercise of the consolidate-into-sulis v0.1.0 methodology; recipe-improvement signals captured for v0.1.1. Next: sulis-security → sulis. v1.77.0 landed Phase 3 prep of the change-as-primitive build — authors the `consolidate-into-sulis` meta-skill (sulis v0.34.0) via add-skill v0.7.0. Codifies the proven sulis-execution → sulis migration recipe (5 commits + 2 verification gates; Gate 0 inventory/plan/code-health-baseline; Commits 1–5 move scripts/skills/agents/refs/wrap-up; Gate 6 code-health verification with rollback-vs-fix-forward threshold at >3 fix-forward commits). Ships 4 deterministic helper scripts (inventory.py JSON, detect_collisions.py + find_external_refs.py + compare_baseline.py Markdown) with a tin-test rename pass on incoming founder-visible skill names (bare verbs like `decompose` / `harden` / `probe` flagged; renamed `decompose` → `plan-work`). Independence Check via Agent subagent_type=Explore in fresh context returned Verdict: PASS across all 8 dimensions with 5 advisory refinements (all applied). Foundation for Phase 3 proper — the four upcoming consolidations (sulis-context → sulis-security → sea → srd, smallest first as practice). v1.76.0 landed Phase 2 of the change-as-primitive build — rewrites the front-door agent via add-agent v0.1.0 (concierge → Sulis rename; COACHING + TONE embedded; dual-register pattern applied with /sulis:jargon on|off toggle; new Coach + Invoker + Partner role articulation across the six-stage journey). Cross-plugin operational references updated (.concierge/ → .sulis/; /sulis-execution: → /sulis:; subagent_type sulis-execution:* → bare names). Deprecated sulis-concierge plugin shell removed (the v0.2.0 deprecation period closed at v0.33.0). Restart Claude session with `claude --agent sulis` to feel the new tone stack + dual-register behaviour. v1.75.0 lands Phase 1 of the change-as-primitive build — authors the `add-agent` meta-skill (sulis v0.32.0) via add-skill v0.7.0, mirroring its five-gate structure with three agent-specific differences (Gate 2 register declaration + dispatch contract; Gate 4 Coaching Delivery + Tone Conformance + Register Switch Correctness perspectives for founder-facing agents; Gate 5 MUC-A1..A4 + MUC-R1..R3 misuse cases). Independence Check via Agent subagent_type=Explore in fresh context returned Verdict: PASS across all dimensions. Foundation for Phase 2 (Sulis agent rewrite — concierge → Sulis with COACHING + TONE embedded and dual-register applied). v1.74.0 lands Phase 0 of the change-as-primitive design (plugins/sulis/docs/change-as-primitive-design.md) — ports COACHING_STANDARD + TONE_STANDARD from the platform into plugins/sulis/references/standards/ (now 8 standards across methodology + founder-communication tiers); adds Rule 6 (Dual register: default founder, on-request technical via /sulis:jargon on|off) to founder-facing-conventions.md. Foundation for Phase 1 (add-agent meta-skill) and Phase 2 (Sulis agent rewrite). v1.73.0 consolidates sulis-execution into the canonical sulis plugin (v0.30.0) — 50 files moved (3429-LOC _wpxlib + 11 wpx-* CLIs + 6 skills + 2 agents + 3 references + 249 tests + 8 research docs + CI workflow + cross-plugin doc updates) across 5 commits (02c1e77 / 6621e5b / 99607e8 / 5278a85 / this wrap-up). Founders now reach the executor surface natively via /sulis:run-wp WP-NNN, /sulis:run-all, /sulis:retry WP-NNN, /sulis:wp-status (renamed from status to avoid the sulis:status concierge-journey conflict), /sulis:backfill-code-review, /sulis:backfill-gates. sulis-execution shell retained at v0.25.0 as a [DEPRECATED] SDK distribution wrapper — the SDK packages (sulis-execution on PyPI, @sulis-ai/execution on npm, sulis-execution-mcp on PyPI) keep stable published names; will move to a dedicated sulis-ai/sulis-execution-sdk repo separately. No deprecation shim skills (mirrors the sulis-concierge → sulis precedent at v0.2.0). All 249 executor tests still pass; cross-skill self-test confirmed 0 findings on the migrated tree. v1.56.0 rewrites sulis:add-skill to v0.7.0 — standards-grounded methodology citing the five v1.55.0 standards at each of the five gates. Gate 1 (Find) adopts Balanced Investigation / Source Independence / Confidence Calibration + adds Primitive Discovery sub-step grounded in PG-01..04 + PD-01..06. Gate 2 (Scope Lock) adopts STANDARDS_RUBRIC phase classification + Outside-In Reasoning + MECE; new lock items: standards-phase classification, verification tier, tool stack (audit-pattern skills must declare which real tools they invoke — Semgrep / Gitleaks / Trivy / lizard / jscpd / hadolint / testssl / curl — pure-regex requires explicit justification), related skills (per REFERENTIAL_INTEGRITY). Gate 3 (Generate) adopts MECE + Pyramid + SCQA + No Hyperbole linguistic audit. Gate 4 (Evaluate) adopts SPIRAL_TEMPLATES tier + dimensions + Codebase Referential Integrity + VERIFICATION_REPORT.md on disk (single filesystem check determines compliance). Gate 5 (Adversarial) adopts AT-01..03 + Independence Check for HEAVY-tier skills (Agent subagent_type=Explore in fresh context). Skills are deep + thorough, never fast — the fast-vs-deep framing rejected. Three modes: greenfield (new skill), deepening / upsurge (extending an existing skill), standards-grounded re-author (pre-v0.7.0 → v0.7.0). COMPLETENESS_REPORT.md renamed to VERIFICATION_REPORT.md (aligned with SPIRAL_TEMPLATES). v1.55.0 ships five cross-cutting standards inside the sulis plugin (plugins/sulis/references/standards/) — Critical Thinking (13 principles + 9 anti-patterns), Decomposition Procedure (PD-01..PD-06), Spiral Templates (LIGHT/STANDARD/HEAVY tiers + ACCA + Codebase Referential Integrity + Independence Check + VERIFICATION_REPORT.md template), Standards Rubric (input/processing/output/governance phase classification), Referential Integrity (4 canonical relationship types + RI-01..05). Adapted from the platform methodology standards (CRITICAL_THINKING_STANDARD v1.5.0, decomposition-procedure v1.0.0, SPIRAL_TEMPLATES v2.1.0, STANDARDS_RUBRIC v1.0.0, REFERENTIAL_INTEGRITY_STANDARD v1.0.1) and trimmed for sulis scale — Wired Outcomes Registry dropped, 26-standard inventory reduced to 5 sulis-local entries, ACCA inlined into Spiral Templates so the port is self-contained. Foundation for the upsurge plan in /Users/iain/.claude/plans/eager-crunching-quail.md — Phase 1 rewrites add-skill v0.7.0 to cite these standards at each gate (replaces ad-hoc methodology with platform-proven rigor); Phase 2 upsurges each check-* skill against the methodology + scores under the spiral rubric. v1.19.0 adds sulis-business-strategy (Kind-schema variant of the business-strategy studio, v0.1.0 test fixture): ships the Identity Kind — a four-stage Kind manifest (find → generate → evaluate → decide) that replaces the legacy identity-articulation outcome's three-lens triad with a single evaluate stage + rubric + structured Verdict + reason-class routing. The triad's function (multi-perspective evaluation with consensus) is preserved structurally in evaluate + decide; its mechanism (three parallel agent lanes) is replaced. Coexists with the established sulis-strategy plugin. Until the platform engine wires Slices 2-4 of the Kind compiler, the plugin's business-strategist agent drives all four stages manually; when the engine catches up, the agent simplifies to invoking the Kind via KindHandler with no Kind YAML change. Test fixture for promoting the migration pattern to studios. v1.16.0 publishes the Repository Contract Standard (RC-01..RC-13) at plugins/sulis/references/repository-contract-standard.md — the marketplace-wide specification of the GitHub repository shape every Sulis agent assumes. Locks: dev/main two-branch model, branch protections, GitHub Merge Queue config on dev (group size 5, concurrency 3, failure tolerance 3 per the Shopify pattern), six required Actions workflows (branch-ci on pull_request, merge-queue-ci on merge_group, deploy-staging on push to dev, health-and-smoke on workflow_run, promote-dev-to-main on workflow_dispatch, release-prod on push to main), staging + production environments, per-env deploy tokens, squash-only repo settings, v* SemVer tag protection, token scopes, and the agent arrival check (executor Step 0) with structured JSON output. Strict mode for v1: agents refuse non-conforming repos and surface a delta list in founder English. Composes with GIT-01..GIT-10 (operationalises the two-branch model on GitHub), EL-01..EL-08 (adds Step 0 to the executor lifecycle), and the cicd-batching-analysis.md research (the merge-queue config encodes its per-batch deploy recommendation). Companion scripts wpx-arrival-check + wpx-bootstrap-repo specified in RC-11 and the Bootstrap Workflow section; implementation scheduled for the next sulis-execution release. The cicd-batching-analysis.md technical research ships alongside at plugins/sulis-execution/docs/research/ documenting the merge-queue convention (Bors → Shopify Shipit → GitHub Merge Queue) and SEA's natural batch unit (DAG topological levels). All v1.15.x capabilities preserved.

plugins.name: srd
plugins.description: [DEPRECATED at v1.23.0 — consolidated into sulis at v0.37.0] Requirements Analyst functionality now lives at /sulis:codebase-mapping, /sulis:critical-thinking, /sulis:requirements-validation, /sulis:index-specifications, /sulis:requirements-templates, /sulis:map-architecture with the requirements-analyst agent at plugins/sulis/agents/. Marketplace-wide standards (AAF, FE, repository-contract, pr-hygiene, change-work, convention-preference, engineering-principles, executor-loop, git-workflow, security, cognitive-load, content-quality, coaching-without-conflict) moved to plugins/sulis/references/. This plugin shell remains for marketplace compatibility; no new development.

plugins.name: sulis-context
plugins.description: [DEPRECATED at v0.4.0 — consolidated into sulis at v0.35.0] Context Cartographer functionality now lives at /sulis:discover-context, /sulis:refresh-context, /sulis:show-context with the context-cartographer agent in plugins/sulis/agents/. This plugin shell remains for marketplace compatibility; no new development.

plugins.name: sulis-strategy
plugins.description: Business strategy studio for the Outcome-First Methodology. Provides vision, strategy, principles, commercial, gtm-planning, roadmap, identity, brand-research, company-research, competitive-research, anti-goals, and win-loss-analysis skills.

plugins.name: sulis-business-strategy
plugins.description: Business Strategy studio (Kind-schema variant). Drives business-context intake, identity, brand positioning, voice, principles, and strategic foundation work through four-stage Kind workflows (find / generate / evaluate / decide).

plugins.name: sulis-product-development
plugins.description: Product development studio for the Outcome-First Methodology. Provides design, plan, implement, and complete skills for the full product delivery lifecycle. Includes test-scenarios, task-decomposition, backend/frontend development guidance, and IVS-authoring.

plugins.name: sulis-builder
plugins.description: Self-service studio creation for the Outcome-First Methodology. Creates new domain expertise packages (7-file studio bundles) using the studio-creation sequence; outcome-definition + platform integration + tail outcomes.

plugins.name: sulis-design
plugins.description: Design studio for the Outcome-First Methodology. Provides design foundation, visual identity, customer experience, design coherence, design compliance, identity articulation, and implementation system skills for translating brand into a design system.

plugins.name: sulis-platform-sdk
plugins.description: Build production-ready, business-critical applications with AI-native infrastructure. Deploy scalable SaaS backends with built-in auth, billing, multi-tenancy, and observability.

plugins.name: sea
plugins.description: [DEPRECATED at v0.21.0 — consolidated into sulis at v0.38.0] Architect skills (draft-architecture, plan-work, harden-codebase, analyse-codebase, verify-architecture, code-review, codebase-audit, suggest-split) + engineering-architect agent + 11 references (boring-code, change-primitives, code-review-standard, mece-3-architecture, red-green-blue, etc.) now in plugins/sulis/. Shell preserved for compatibility.

plugins.name: sulis-security
plugins.description: [DEPRECATED at v0.7.0 — consolidated into sulis at v0.40.0] Security-reviewer agent + viability-framework reference now in plugins/sulis/. codebase-assess skill preserved at /sulis:codebase-assess through its existing deprecation window (skill was already DEPRECATED in favour of /sulis:code-health since v0.22.0). Shell preserved for compatibility.

plugins.name: idc
plugins.description: Investor Deck Coach — facilitates Sequoia-style pitch deck creation through guided conversation, stage-aware (angel through Series B), with rigorous market research, financial modelling, adversarial investor-objection review, branded deck deliverables (PowerPoint + Reveal.js HTML), and rehearsal drill.

plugins.name: sulis-execution
plugins.description: [DEPRECATED — see /sulis:*] Executor + train + wpx-* CLI tools moved into the sulis plugin at v0.30.0 (2026-05-25). Use /sulis:run-wp WP-NNN, /sulis:run-all, /sulis:retry WP-NNN, /sulis:wp-status (renamed from status to avoid sulis:status conflict with the concierge journey status), /sulis:backfill-code-review, /sulis:backfill-gates. This plugin shell retained for the SDK packages only: sdk/python (sulis-execution on PyPI), sdk/typescript (@sulis-ai/execution on npm), sdk/mcp-server (sulis-execution-mcp on PyPI). SDK packages keep stable names because external consumers depend on them; will move to a dedicated sulis-ai/sulis-execution-sdk repo separately.

plugins.name: sulis
plugins.description: Sulis — the canonical front-door plugin for the Sulis AI marketplace. Hosts the founder-facing Sulis agent, journey skills, three meta-skills, eight cross-cutting standards, the deep-mode code-health framework, the address-findings bridge, and the executor + train + wpx-* CLI tools. Founders interact via /sulis:* commands. Longer-term direction is sulis as the only plugin shipped — see plugins/sulis/docs/change-as-primitive-design.md. See CHANGELOG.md for version history.
